title: hahnia torminalis
intro: Hahnia torminalis, also known as the wild mandrake or mandrake, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Hahnia torminalis include relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and stomach cramps, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce anxiety. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it's essential to note that Hahnia torminalis can be toxic in large quantities and should be used under the guidance of a medical professional.
